stories.headline: Tremors felt in Delhi, parts of North India as earthquake jolts Afghanistan
stories.summary: A 6.1 magnitude earthquake struck Afghanistan, causing tremors to be felt across Delhi and parts of north India. The epicentre of the quake was 241 kilometres north-northeast of Kabul, and it occurred at 2:50 pm IST. The National Centre for Seismology reported the earthquake, which sent brief waves of panic among residents. Tremors were also felt in other parts of north India, including Jammu and Kashmir. Additionally, a 5.8 magnitude quake hit southern Afghanistan, and a 6.6 magnitude quake struck 133 km southeast of Fayzabad in Afghanistan, both causing tremors in Delhi-NCR and other parts of north India. The earthquakes occurred on different days, with the most recent one causing tremors in the national capital and surrounding areas. The Meteorological Centre Srinagar and National Center for Seismology reported the earthquakes.

stories.headline: 5.9 magnitude earthquake hits Afghanistan's Hindu Kush region, tremors felt in north India
stories.summary: A 5.9 magnitude earthquake struck Afghanistan's Hindu Kush region, with tremors felt across northern India, including Delhi-NCR. The epicentre of the earthquake was near Jurm in Afghanistan, close to the Afghanistan-Tajikistan border. The quake occurred in a highly seismically active zone, where the Indian and Eurasian tectonic plates collide. At least 8 people were killed in the earthquake. Strong tremors were felt in several parts of North India, triggering panic and causing residents to rush out of homes and office buildings. The earthquake is part of a series of tremors that have hit Asian regions in recent days. The Hindu Kush mountain range, where the earthquake occurred, is prone to seismic activity due to its location along the collision zone between the two tectonic plates.

stories.headline: US deports eight people 'of African origin' to Uganda
stories.summary: The United States has deported eight people of African origin to Uganda, marking the first transfer under a migration deal signed between the two countries. The deportees are from different African countries and were accepted by Uganda as they did not have criminal histories. This transfer is part of the 2025 Safe Third Country Deal, which allows the US to deport migrants to Uganda if they are not from the country but passed through it. The group consists of third-country nationals, and their deportation is seen as a significant development in the migration agreement between the US and Uganda. The Ugandan government has confirmed receiving the deportees, and the transfer is expected to pave the way for similar deportations in the future. The deal aims to address migration issues between the two countries.

stories.headline: UNIFIL says 3 peacekeepers wounded in Lebanon
stories.summary: The United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) reported that three of its peacekeepers were wounded in a blast at one of its positions in southern Lebanon on Friday. Two of the peacekeepers were seriously injured and are being evacuated to a hospital. This incident marks the third such attack in a week, amid escalating hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah. The blast occurred near the Israeli border, where tensions have been rising due to the ongoing US-Israel conflict with Iran. UNIFIL vehicles have been operating in the area, and the force has been working to maintain stability in the region. The incident is the latest in a series of attacks on UNIFIL positions, highlighting the increasing volatility of the situation in southern Lebanon.

stories.headline: Major Galaxy S26 FE leak reveals its chip and performance A new leak has revealed the processor and performance of the Samsung Galaxy S26 FE, which is expected to launch in the second half of 2026.
stories.summary: The Samsung Galaxy S26 FE has surfaced on Geekbench, revealing key specs. The device, with model number SM-S741U, is expected to launch in the second half of 2026. According to the listing, the Galaxy S26 FE will be powered by an Exynos 2500 chip and run on Android 17. The leak was spotted by tipster Abhishek Yadav, who believes the model number corroborates the unannounced Samsung Galaxy S26 FE. The Geekbench listing provides early insight into the device's performance and specifications. The Galaxy S26 FE is expected to arrive with a proprietary Exynos chipset, and rumors have surfaced about its supposed chip performance results and other features, including camera and battery specifications. The leak confirms some of these rumors, but more information is expected to be revealed as the launch date approaches.

stories.headline: Apple iPhone 18 pro series set for September launch with big upgrades
stories.summary: Apple's iPhone 18 Pro series is set to launch in September 2026, featuring significant upgrades including the A20 Pro chip, a bigger battery, and improved camera. The Pro and Pro Max models will be released alongside the iPhone Fold. However, the standard iPhone 18 models are expected to be delayed until spring 2027, with Apple potentially splitting the launch into phases. The iPhone 18 Pro models are rumored to drop the Dynamic Island feature. The upcoming series is scheduled to be unveiled at Apple's annual release event in September, with the Pro and Pro Max variants taking center stage. The exact release date, price, and specifications of the iPhone 18 series are yet to be confirmed, but the upcoming launch is highly anticipated. The iPhone 18 series is expected to bring notable improvements to the iPhone lineup.

stories.headline: HER2 heterogeneity in breast cancer may drive T-DXd resistance and recurrence, with ABCC1 and USP9X emerging as promising therapeutic targets.
stories.summary: Researchers have identified HER2 heterogeneity in breast cancer as a potential driver of resistance and recurrence to the treatment T-DXd. Studies have shown that T-DXd can promote the regrowth of HER2-positive cells, leading to tumor recurrence. The findings, published in Cancer Discovery, suggest that targeting ABCC1 and USP9X may be a promising therapeutic approach. Additionally, combining mitotic inhibitors with other treatments may elicit synergistic antitumor effects. HER2-targeted therapy has improved outcomes for HER2-positive breast cancer patients, but benefits remain uneven. The study reveals that targeting both AXL and HER2 could overcome trastuzumab resistance, improving treatment outcomes. These discoveries provide new insights into the dynamics of HER2-positive breast cancer and potential strategies to overcome resistance to targeted therapies. The research highlights the complexity of breast cancer and the need for personalized treatment approaches.

stories.headline: Morbid Metal launches in Early Access on April 8 Roguelike hack-and-slash action game Morbid Metal will launch in Early Access for PC via Steam on April 8 for a limited-time launch price of $13.49 / €13.49…
stories.summary: Morbid Metal, a roguelike hack-and-slash action game, will launch in Early Access on PC via Steam on April 8. The game will be available at a limited-time launch price of $13.49 / €13.49, which will increase to $17.99 / €17.99 after the initial promotion. Players who purchase the game between April 8-20 will receive a 25% discount. Developed by SCREEN JUICE and published by Ubisoft, Morbid Metal allows players to explore a post-apocalyptic world, taking on enemies with stylish combos and powerful abilities. The Early Access launch will provide players with an opportunity to experience the game before its full release, with the limited-time discount offering an incentive to join early. The game can be wishlisted on Steam ahead of its April 8 launch.

stories.headline: Rute Cardoso, Widow of Diogo Jota, Revealed the Last Message She Sent to Her Husband Before the Accident and Death
stories.summary: Diogo Jota, the late Liverpool football player, received a final message from his wife, Rute Cardoso, before his fatal car accident. Rute sent a text message to Diogo just moments before the crash, expressing her desire to send him their wedding video. Unaware of the accident, she had asked him to wait until he could pull over to receive the footage. Diogo and Rute had been married for just 11 days before his tragic death in a car accident last July, which also claimed the life of his brother, Andre Silva. Rute has now shared the heartbreaking final message she sent to her husband, revealing the last communication they had before the accident. The message has been disclosed as a poignant reminder of the couple's love and the tragic circumstances of Diogo's death.

stories.headline: Wellington Phoenix Women clinch semi-final spot with win over Adelaide
stories.summary: Wellington Phoenix Women have secured a semi-final spot with a 2-0 win over Adelaide United at Coopers Stadium. The team has surged to the second spot on the A-League ladder. Earlier, they achieved a 3-1 victory over Adelaide United in Porirua, ending Adelaide's five-game unbeaten run. Two goals in three minutes gave Wellington Phoenix a 2-0 lead at half-time. The team, led by head coach Bev Priestman, is competing in the A-League Women in the 2025-26 season. With this win, Wellington Phoenix will face Adelaide United again in the final round of the regular season on Good Friday, where a win can secure second spot on the A-League Women table. The semi-final spot marks a significant milestone in the team's fifth season in the A-League Women.

stories.headline: Rodgers comments on reports regarding Renard’s succession as Saudi Arabia manager
stories.summary: Brendan Rodgers, manager of Al-Qadsia, has commented on reports linking him to the role of Saudi Arabia national team manager, potentially succeeding Frenchman Hervé Renard. Rodgers' comments come amid speculation about his future and the possibility of taking over the Saudi team. The development occurs as Saudi Arabia navigates economic challenges, including a forecasted $26.8 billion deficit for 2025 and a slow economic growth rate of 0.8 percent. The country recently issued its largest-ever bond sale of $12 billion to support mega projects. Rodgers' potential appointment would be significant, given the current state of Saudi Arabia's relations with the West and its impact on regional and global security concerns, including Islamist terrorism. The Irish manager's statement is his first public response to the rumors surrounding Renard's succession.

stories.headline: Dan Orlovsky cries on air as autistic son brightens ESPN broadcast
stories.summary: Dan Orlovsky was brought to tears on ESPN's "NFL Live" as he introduced his 14-year-old autistic son, Madden, in recognition of World Autism Awareness Day. Madden joined his father on-air, showcasing his drawings and singing the Philadelphia Eagles' fight song, "Fly Eagles Fly." The emotional moment highlighted Madden's passion for the Eagles and his artistic talents. This was not Madden's first appearance on the program, having taken part a year ago. The studio show spotlighted his artwork and shared a heartfelt message for his family, making for a memorable and emotional broadcast. Dan Orlovsky's emotional reaction was a testament to the special bond between him and his son. The appearance has gone viral, with many praising the heartwarming moment and the awareness it brings to autism.

stories.headline: Tremors felt in Delhi, parts of North India as earthquake jolts Afghanistan
stories.summary: A 6.1 magnitude earthquake struck Afghanistan, causing tremors to be felt across Delhi and parts of north India. The epicentre of the quake was 241 kilometres north-northeast of Kabul, and it occurred at 2:50 pm IST. The National Centre for Seismology reported the earthquake, which sent brief waves of panic among residents. Tremors were also felt in other parts of north India, including Jammu and Kashmir. Additionally, a 5.8 magnitude quake hit southern Afghanistan, and a 6.6 magnitude quake struck 133 km southeast of Fayzabad in Afghanistan, causing tremors in Delhi-NCR and other areas. The earthquakes occurred on different days, with the most recent one causing widespread tremors in the region. The Meteorological Centre Srinagar and National Center for Seismology reported the earthquakes, providing details on their magnitude and epicentres.

stories.headline: 5.9-Magnitude Earthquake Hits Afghanistan, Tremors Felt In Delhi-NCR
stories.summary: A 5.9-magnitude earthquake struck Afghanistan's Hindu Kush region, causing strong tremors across parts of North India, including Delhi-NCR, Punjab, and Haryana. The epicentre of the earthquake was near Darawan at 71.01 degrees longitude and 36.52 degrees latitude. The quake occurred late Friday, resulting in at least eight deaths in northern and eastern Afghanistan, as well as western Pakistan. Residents across Delhi-NCR felt the tremors, with reports of strong earthquake tremors also coming from other parts of North India. The earthquake's impact was felt across the region, with multiple countries experiencing the effects of the quake. The earthquake's magnitude was initially reported as 5.8, but was later confirmed to be 5.9.

stories.headline: 5.9 magnitude earthquake hits Afghanistan's Hindu Kush region, tremors felt in north India
stories.summary: A 5.9 magnitude earthquake struck Afghanistan's Hindu Kush region, with tremors felt in parts of India, including Delhi-NCR, Punjab, Haryana, Jammu and Kashmir, and Kargil. The quake occurred at a depth of 75 km, according to the National Centre for Seismology. In Pakistan, the earthquake was felt in cities and towns such as Islamabad, Peshawar, Chitral, Swat, and Shangla, as reported by the Pakistan Meteorological Department. The USGS confirmed the earthquake's magnitude and epicentre. The tremors were reported on Wednesday morning, with local media reporting the quake's impact in various regions. The earthquake's effects were felt across northern India, with Delhi being one of the affected areas. The quake's impact was also reported in other parts of the region, causing widespread tremors.

stories.headline: Teen stabs man to death in Delhi, boasts about his crime in social media video
stories.summary: A 20-year-old man was stabbed to death in northwest Delhi following a quarrel. The accused, 18-year-old Karan Kumar, then posted a video on Instagram boasting about the murder. Kumar, who describes himself as a "photographer and visual storyteller," shared the video after committing the crime. The incident occurred at 11:22 pm, and Kumar's social media post was later discovered by authorities. Police have taken note of the video and are investigating the case. The victim and the circumstances surrounding the quarrel that led to the stabbing have not been fully disclosed. The case is currently under investigation, with Kumar's social media post being used as evidence.

stories.headline: Why Losing the Iran War May Be the Best Outcome for the Global Economy
stories.summary: Iran's threat to disrupt global oil supplies has sparked concerns of a "doomsday" scenario for energy markets. The country has warned it can send oil prices to $200 a barrel by targeting shipping and refineries in the Middle East. Recent attacks, including Iranian missiles hitting a Qatari liquefied natural gas facility, have heightened tensions. The US response has been mixed, with President Trump declaring the war "won" while also suggesting it could escalate further, creating uncertainty for global markets. Analysts warn that the conflict has no economic upside, and the recovery process will be slow even under the best circumstances. Businesses and investors are struggling to respond to the situation, with markets experiencing violent swings due to the lack of clarity from Washington. The potential halt of Gulf energy exports could have severe global economic consequences.

stories.headline: The Iran war shatters the ‘axis’ myth in West Asia
stories.summary: The ongoing war against Iran has dismantled the theory of rigid alliances in West Asia, revealing that strategic interests in the region are more fluid than previously thought. The 33-day conflict has shattered the myth of military supremacy, forcing a reevaluation of security assumptions in the region. The wars of 2025 and 2026 have stripped away longstanding assumptions about regional security, highlighting the complexity of alliances and interests in West Asia. The conflict has also drawn comparisons to past military setbacks, including echoes of the "Black Hawk Down" incident. The shifting landscape of West Asian security has significant implications for regional actors and their strategies, marking a significant shift away from the traditional "axis" narrative.

stories.headline: Can a War 3,000 Km Away Rewrite Kerala’s Election Mood?
stories.summary: The ongoing conflict in West Asia, 3,000 km away, is significantly impacting Kerala's election mood. Families in Malappuram, Thrissur, and Kozhikode are worried about the safety and job stability of their loved ones working in the region. Kerala's economy, heavily reliant on remittances, may face challenges. The conflict has also affected Non-Resident Keralites (NRKs) returning to vote in the April 9 elections, with flight disruptions, soaring airfares, and regional uncertainty. Kerala has 2,34,049 overseas voters, with 2.2 million expatriates living in West Asia. Chartered flights are being arranged to facilitate their return. The war is also drying up party funds and altering the upcoming Assembly polls, with flight cancellations and economic disruptions posing challenges to traditional election dynamics. The situation has become a major concern in the state ahead of the elections.

stories.headline: Structural energy shortages, not geopolitics alone, are driving global conflict, and a U.S. defeat in Iran could accelerate a necessary economic reorganization
stories.summary: The US and Iran have reached a stalemate, with further escalation likely to lead to catastrophe, according to Iran expert Ali Vaez. A US ground invasion of Iran could cause a global economic meltdown. The Iran war, now in its second month, has severely constrained shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Senior US military officials have informed a key Middle East ally that President Donald Trump may authorize a US attack on Iran. Despite recent US-Israeli strikes, Iran's national electricity grid remains stable, with power restored in Tehran and other affected areas. The conflict has significant implications for global trade, with the Bab-el-Mandeb Strait being a critical transit point for oil, LNG, wheat, rice, and fertilizer shipments. The US and its allies are weighing their next moves, with potential consequences for the global economy.

stories.headline: Kapil Sibal represents Mamata govt in SC after mob violence in Muslim-dominated Malda: Read about his past record
stories.summary: Senior Advocate Kapil Sibal is representing the Mamata Banerjee government in the Supreme Court after a mob violence incident in Malda, West Bengal. The incident occurred on April 1, where a huge mob gheraoed seven judicial officers, including three women judges, for over 9 hours at the Block Development Officer office in Muslim-dominated Kaliachak. The officers were working on the Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls. Sibal sought to remove the phrase "complete breakdown of law and order" from the order, calling it an isolated incident. The case has sparked controversy, with allegations of the state government's nonfeasance and the involvement of a Muslim mob instigated by a former TMC leader. Sibal's representation of the TMC government has raised eyebrows, given his past record of representing controversial cases, including the Ayodhya Ram Mandir case and the 2G spectrum scam case.

stories.headline: Biden Administration Faces Criticism Over Handling of Classified Documents Scandal
stories.summary: The Biden Administration is facing criticism over its handling of a classified documents scandal. On January 9, 2023, attorneys for President Joe Biden discovered classified government documents in his former office. The discovery sparked an investigation, with the FBI subpoenaing phone records of individuals connected to the case. The controversy has led to questions about the administration's transparency and record-keeping practices. A congressional committee has also raised concerns about the president's cognitive decline and potential cover-ups. The issue has drawn comparisons to investigations into former President Donald Trump's handling of classified documents. The Biden Administration's response to the scandal has been scrutinized, with some arguing that it has not been forthcoming enough about the discovery and handling of the classified documents.

stories.headline: US Senate Votes to Block Biden's Vaccine Mandate for Healthcare Workers
stories.summary: The US Senate voted to block President Biden's vaccine mandate for healthcare workers, with a 49-44 vote in favor of a resolution of disapproval under the Congressional Review Act (CRA). The resolution, which had bipartisan support, aims to eliminate the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services' rule requiring healthcare workers to receive a COVID-19 vaccine. Senators who supported the resolution argued that the mandate is an overreach of presidential power and could lead to staffing shortages in rural hospitals. The resolution will now go to the House of Representatives. Several senators, including John Cornyn, Bill Hagerty, and Steve Daines, spoke out in favor of the resolution, citing concerns about individual choice and the potential impact on healthcare workers and patient care.

stories.headline: European Union Leaders Meet to Discuss Russia-Ukraine Conflict and Economic Sanctions
stories.summary: European Union leaders met in Brussels to discuss the Russia-Ukraine conflict and economic sanctions. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy joined the summit, urging the EU to impose further sanctions on Russia and follow through with its frozen assets proposal. The meeting aimed to address long-term funding for Kyiv and strategies to counter potential US tariffs. The EU and US had recently imposed a fresh round of sanctions on Russia. The leaders also discussed the implementation of the bloc's new migration policy, which has faced setbacks. Zelenskyy expressed optimism that an agreement on sanctions was close, emphasizing that Russia must pay the price for its invasion. The summit was part of the European Political Community, which brings together EU members and 20 other countries to respond to the Russian invasion of Ukraine. The EU seeks to hit Russia economically through targeted sanctions.

stories.headline: Stock Market Holiday Today: Are Sensex, Nifty Closed On Good Friday
stories.summary: The Indian stock market is closed today, April 3, on account of Good Friday. Domestic stock exchanges, including the National Stock Exchange (NSE) and Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE), are shut for trading. This is the second stock market holiday this month. Most bank branches are also closed, but online banking services such as ATMs, UPI, net banking, and mobile banking remain operational. The closure is in observance of Good Friday, a significant holy day for Christians worldwide. As a result, trading activities on major stock exchanges, including Sensex and Nifty, have been suspended for the day. Normal trading is expected to resume on the next working day.

stories.headline: Stock markets worldwide are under strain due to the Iran war with the US and Israel
stories.summary: Global stock markets are experiencing strain due to the escalating conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran. The S&P 500 posted a weekly gain despite being impacted by the Middle East war signals, following its worst quarter since 2022. Geopolitical tensions are affecting energy infrastructure, increasing the risk of supply disruptions and keeping oil and gas prices high. The conflict is also threatening global food security and petrochemical supply. Traders had previously driven a two-day rally in the S&P 500 amid fears of escalation, but markets have since paused. The situation remains volatile, with oil markets reacting cautiously to claims of a potential ceasefire, and the ongoing conflict continues to impact markets and commodity prices worldwide. The US-Israel war with Iran is causing far-reaching effects on global markets and economies.

stories.headline: Indian Stock Market Plunges Amid Global Recession Fears and Geopolitical Tensions
stories.summary: The Indian stock market has plummeted amid rising global recession fears and escalating geopolitical tensions. The benchmark indices, Sensex and Nifty, have witnessed significant downturns, with the Sensex crashing nearly 1,600 points, a 2% fall. This extends a streak of losses over several trading sessions. The Indian rupee has also hit a record low, crossing 95 against the US dollar for the first time. Global markets, including the Dow and Nasdaq, have also fallen, with the Dow losing 2% and the Nasdaq losing 2% last week, and the S&P 500 losing 1.5%. The ongoing Iran conflict and oil price fears are contributing to the market volatility, resulting in investor wealth loss and increased uncertainty on Dalal Street. The downturn is part of a broader global market trend, with investors reacting to rising geopolitical tensions.

stories.headline: BSE and NSE to Remain Closed Today Due to Good Friday Holiday
stories.summary: Indian stock markets, including the BSE and NSE, are closed today, April 3, 2026, due to the Good Friday holiday. All trading and settlement activities, such as equities, derivatives, and the Securities Lending and Borrowing segment, are suspended for the day. The Multi Commodity Exchange of India and the National Commodity & Derivatives Exchange are also shut. Trading will resume on Monday, April 6. This is the first stock market holiday of April 2026 and the fifth trading holiday of the year. Indian stock markets are scheduled to remain closed for 20 days in 2026, with 16 of these holidays falling on regular working days. The closure is as per the official holiday schedule issued by the exchanges, and it marks a shorter trading week for investors.

stories.headline: OpenAI buys tech talk show TBPN as it builds out communication strategy
stories.summary: OpenAI has acquired the online technology talk show TBPN for an undisclosed sum. The move is part of OpenAI's effort to build out its communication strategy and drive conversations around AI. TBPN will continue to operate and promote the business of technology, sitting within OpenAI's strategy organization. The acquisition marks OpenAI's first foray into media ownership, raising questions about editorial independence. The show will maintain its focus on discussing technology and business, with OpenAI's support. The exact terms of the deal and how OpenAI plans to integrate TBPN into its overall strategy remain unclear. The acquisition has drawn attention from the media and technology industries, with many watching to see how OpenAI will balance its ownership with the need for editorial independence. TBPN's continued operation is expected to provide a platform for discussing AI and technology.

stories.headline: Google Unveils PaLM 2, a More Powerful AI Model
stories.summary: Google has unveiled PaLM 2, its latest Large Language Model (LLM), which boasts advanced capabilities in reasoning, coding, and mathematics. Released on October 15, 2025, PaLM 2 is designed to help businesses create powerful AI tools. The model brings significant advancements in multilingual support and reasoning abilities. However, in a recent update, Google has replaced PaLM 2 with Gemini, its most powerful model to date, to power its chatbot Bard. Additionally, Google has introduced Gemma 4, an open model family built for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, delivering high-performance results. These updates demonstrate Google's ongoing efforts to improve its AI capabilities and provide businesses with more powerful tools. The introduction of PaLM 2 and subsequent updates aim to enhance the development of AI-powered applications.

stories.headline: Meta Releases Llama, an AI Designed to Process and Generate Human-Like Language
stories.summary: Meta has released Llama 4, a new collection of AI models in its Llama family, including Llama 4 Scout, Llama 4 Maverick, and Llama 4 Behemoth. The company introduced Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, which are open-weight natively multimodal models with unprecedented context support, built using a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture. These models are part of Meta's large language model (LLM) and are designed to process and generate human-like language. The release was announced on a Saturday, catching some AI experts off guard. Llama 4 models are built on one of the world's most advanced LLMs, according to Meta. The new models aim to improve language understanding and generation capabilities, with Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick being the first to offer such advanced features.

stories.headline: Microsoft Invests in AI Startup Developing Revolutionary Machine Learning Algorithms
stories.summary: Microsoft has invested in an AI startup that is developing revolutionary machine learning algorithms. The investment is part of Microsoft's broader efforts to drive innovation in AI, with the company committing over $110 billion to build and upgrade AI-optimized data centers. This year, Microsoft is on track to spend $80 billion on AI data centers and model training for its Azure cloud platform. The company has also made significant investments in India, including a $3 billion investment in AI and cloud infrastructure, and plans to train 10 million people in AI. Microsoft's focus on AI infrastructure, scaling, and real-world deployment reflects the industry's shift towards practical applications and collaboration. The investment in the AI startup is expected to contribute to Microsoft's goal of providing customers with cutting-edge AI capabilities.
